message:
fix(mcp-proxy): defer route prefix reservation until after tool mapping

Previously, compose_proxy_tools() reserved a route prefix slot before
attempting to connect and map tools from a remote MCP server. If the
server connection failed (e.g. missing binary), the prefix was still
claimed, blocking any later server that would sanitize to the same
prefix (e.g. "GitHub Copilot" and "GitHub/Copilot" both map to
"github-copilot").

Move the route prefix collision check to after tool mapping succeeds,
so a failed or empty server cannot claim a shared prefix and block a
valid server that connects later in the discovery list.

- Collect mapped handlers into a Vec before inserting the route prefix
- Skip servers that produce zero mounted handlers after local seam mapping
- Add write_multi_discovery_config() and make_proxy_config_with_servers()
  test helpers for multi-server scenarios
- Add proxy_does_not_let_failed_server_claim_shared_route_prefix test
  that verifies the regression fix with two servers sharing a prefix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
